name: HARPE
summary: Set traps with your weapons and punish enemies who move into them with maximized damage and forced movement.
description: When the first humans ascended, some of those who suffered the least dissonance were those who lived as dancers.
activeAbility: Weapon Trap — Throw your weapon up to 6 spaces in any of the four cardinal directions. If you have line of sight to your thrown weapon and a moving enemy breaks it, interrupt the current action. Deal the maximum damage your weapon can do and Pull the enemy any number of spaces you choose toward you or toward your thrown weapon. Your weapon recalls to you when the trap activates or when you spend a Support Action during your turn to retrieve it. When you recall your weapon, you may equip any weapon in your possession as a free action.
passiveAbility: Boundless Dokeo — You may select one additional weapon during STRATCOM.

name: KOPIS
summary: Mark enemies and slay them, then get extra attacks when you move onto their fallen bodies.
description: Carpocratian King Kopis so trusted his senses that before every battle he would gouge out his own eyes, growing them anew again as the bodies of his fallen foes tanned in the sun.
activeAbility: Mag Dump — Mark an enemy within line of sight. When you defeat this enemy, they drop a token. When you step on this token, gain 1 free weapon attack to use immediately.
passiveAbility: Offhand Pistol — If you move on your turn, deal 1D6 damage to each enemy you Provoke. You may Push:1 if you deal 4 or more damage.

name: SHARUR
summary: Create Attractors that pull enemies into them and Break Swarms apart. At low HP, your Attractors become Void, instantly killing enemies who fall into them.
description: These children dance through my mists, they play at my luminous aster. Their gesticulations taught me my own name, and in return, I teach them of attraction.
activeAbility: Back Up — Select a tile within Range:4 and turn it into the Attractor special terrain type. This terrain creates a Range:2 zone around it that inflicts Pull:1 on any creature entering the zone, and an additional Pull:1 on any creature that ends its turn in that zone. This can Break Swarms.
passiveAbility: Emergency Auto Defenses — When you are brought down to 10 HP, all Attractor terrain tiles become Void Attractors. These act like Attractors, but are also Void Terrain. Characters can be Pulled into the Void Attractor and are instantly defeated when this happens.

name: VARUNASTRA
summary: Attack with the patterns of your allies. Grant your squad Transference, letting them heal when they deal damage.
description: Once they bound our hands, now the silver threads are rewoven. Power that was once siphoned into the greedy throats of supplicant parasites now flows between us, joining us—unifying us with all that is, all that was, all that will be.
activeAbility: Borrowing This — As a Main Action, attack using the pattern (but not the damage) of any weapon currently equipped by your squad. If you use their pattern to hit an enemy that could not be hit by your equipped weapon's pattern, you may spend a Support Action to deal maximum weapon damage.
passiveAbility: Gear Check! — Ending your turn adjacent to another player grants them a free weapon-swap action, if they wish. If they choose to swap weapons, they gain Transference:1.

name: HEPHAESTUS
summary: Spend your Equipment use to restore your allies' Equipment uses and finish enemies off with your class ability to refresh your own Equipment.
description: Rapid ascension becomes a valuable weapon in Infernum; the energy produced by a single exalted hellion is more than the atom-breakers burned in all their lifetimes. The release of such limiters is a violent event, focused and carefully shaped into a constructive one.
activeAbility: Synesis Conversion — Deal 1D6 to an enemy within Range:1. If this attack kills the enemy, instantly restore your Equipment usage.
passiveAbility: Baseline Communism — As a free action, you may spend your Equipment to restore an ally's Equipment usage.

name: EPEUS
summary: Bring extra Gear, and if you still don't like what you have, swap it mid-combat.
description: "I know what I need. That's the same as having what I need. One half-shot of Hyper-LSD and my memories become allies in my hands. The bag I remembered packing unfolds in the metasphere, and I have what I need. Always have."
activeAbility: Bag of Tricks — As a Main Action, swap the Gear on your weapon or your armor for another unlocked Gear.
passiveAbility: Deep Pockets — You may select both a Weapon Gear and an Armor Gear during STRATCOM.

name: CHRYSAOR
summary: Punish attacks that aren't aimed directly at you and only you.
description: "Would you like to know the moment you'll die? Would you like it burned on your skin, ticking down with each breath? Would you have it blaze in fire above your head, a true crown of glory declaring the violence of your end to all who would suffer from it? Don't run."
activeAbility: Soul-Branding Demolition Sigil — Mark any one object or creature within line of sight with Brand:2. When the Brand reaches 0, Branded creatures and objects explode, immediately dealing 4D6 damage to the target and 6 damage to all adjacent targets. You can have any number of Brands at a time.
passiveAbility: Embedded Flame Rejection Rituals — You are immune to any Effects (not damage) from attacks or abilities that target an area. You are not immune to any Lingering Effects, nor to Effects from attacks that target you directly have secondary Effects in an area. When you take damage from an attack that targets an area, a pattern, or more than one individual, you may immediately remove 1 stack of Brand from someone you Branded.

name: OGMIOS
summary: Put allies and enemies in your own portable miniature battlefield.
description: The doors are open, but only to those with eyes to see. Hyper-LSD and an adjusted reality-perspective-realignment suite forge walls of golden thought and homunculi of pearls; a bastion for us, a killbox for them.
activeAbility: 59th Level Sophia Transposing Art — Transport one adjacent creature or yourself into your pocket dimension, emerge from your pocket dimension into a square adjacent to its vessel, or eject something in your pocket dimension into an adjacent square.

passiveAbility.name: Sophia Enclosing Hermetic Chamber
passiveAbility.intro: You control a small pocket dimension compressed into a jewel, ritual token, or other small object. Using the 59th Level Sophia Transposing Art, you can transport any active object from the current battlefield into this pocket dimension. The pocket dimension is represented by a 5x5 grid that shares no squares with the main map. When you enter your own pocket dimension, this vessel falls into the square you were in. Allies can carry it while you're within. During combat, when you kill a miniboss or swarm, gain Perspective:1. Spend any number of stacks of Perspective as a free action to alter the properties of your pocket dimension in the following ways:

options: The next friendly unit to enter the dimension gains +1 HP per stack spent until the end of TACCOM.
The next friendly unit to enter the dimension gains +1 Damage to their next attack per stack spent.
Increase your Hermetic Crystal's HP by 1 per stack spent until the end of TACCOM. You may exceed its starting HP.

perspectiveOptions: The next friendly unit to enter the dimension gains +1 HP per stack spent until the end of TACCOM.
The next friendly unit to enter the dimension gains +1 Damage to their next attack per stack spent.
Increase your Hermetic Crystal's HP by 1 per stack spent until the end of TACCOM.

wraith.strike: Deal 2 damage to an adjacent creature
wraith.notes: Wraiths move and act like any other unit, but they are under your control and act either at the beginning or end of your turn rather than on their own turns.

hermeticCrystal.special: If at any point the crystal is destroyed, anything and everything within the pocket dimension is ejected onto free spaces adjacent to the character. If no adjacent space is free, the table can decide where the creatures and objects end up. After the grid is completely empty, reset your grid and replace the Sophia Enclosing Hermetic Crystal.

name: ZOMORROD
summary: Summon copies of your foes to join you in each battle.
description: When I first saw through the gates, I saw the enormity of existence wash over me. I was brought to my knees, hands open, by the weight of it all. I urge you, hold closed your fist and you shall carry a shard of enormity, even should you fall.
activeAbility: Genesis Paradigm — You begin each battle with 3 Charges. As a Support Action, spend a Charge to summon a copy of a minion currently present on the battlefield, or spend 2 Charges to summon a copy of a miniboss currently present on the battlefield. The summoned unit arrives within Range:3 of you and has HP equal to your HP at the time you summon it. It acts immediately after you in the initiative order and may only take a single action per turn. Otherwise, it is identical to the copied miniboss or minion and has all of their abilities and attacks. Minions with the Swarm tag may create a Swarm with the other units you control, gaining the full benefits of that Swarm.
passiveAbility: Dynamic Conciousness Pattern Seals — Whenever you summon a new minion or miniboss, you may Stock it. You can only have one Stocked unit at any one time. This confers two benefits: a Stocked unit may be summoned by expending Charges even when it isn't present; and you may consume the Stock completely to summon that unit as a free action without spending Charges.

name: ULFBERHT
summary: Create Lingering tiles that echo your attacks, then move enemies into them or move the tiles onto them yourself.
description: Ophidian apothecaries devised a salve to seal a wound which would rupture at dawn's call. Fingers Tanned In Lavender observed in her memoirs "wounds, like our worst memories, should echo"
activeAbility: Boundless Abundence — You begin each battle with 5 Charges. When you attack with a weapon, you may spend a Charge to grant that attack Lingering:1. You may spend 3 Charges to include spaces of the weapon effect that already dealt damage in the Lingering Effect. You may spend 2 Charges to move any Lingering space up to three spaces in any direction. It triggers if it moves across an enemy.
passiveAbility: Resanguination — When a non-minion enemy dies, you regain a Charge, up to a maximum of 5 Charges.

name: DERAFSH KAVIANI
summary: Share an extra large HP pool with your allies and grant them extra actions.
description: When Eve-Sophia spread across the skies and took on the 148 Sufferings, humanity looked upon her sprawling and interconnected roots and saw themselves. We are each a dazzling light, bound in the maze of roots and hung from the firmament ever blinding the Demiurge.
activeAbility: Immaterialization Suite — Once per turn, you may select a player character within Range:2 and spend one of your actions to grant that player an immediate use of that type of action. You gain one additional action a step lower than the one you spent—granting another player a Main Action grants you an additional Support Action, granting a Support Action gives you an additional Aux Action. Granting an Aux Action also gives you an additional Aux Action.
passiveAbility: Communist-Aligned Psudosoul Ceramics — You do not have a standard HP pool. In place of it, you begin with 45 Shared HP. Whenever you or a character within Range:2 takes damage, that damage may be subtracted from the Shared HP pool instead of its intended target. Shared HP otherwise acts exactly the same as regular HP. Any healing that targets you recovers Shared HP.
